question: [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a sell offer (offer.buy=false) under the precondition that buyerPrice is not a divisor of targetFilledBuyerAssets*WAD trigger the sequence: (1) pullToken pulls targetBuyerAssets from msg.sender, (2) buyerAssetsToUnits computes units=mulDivDown(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice), (3) Midnight.take executes and returns resBuyerAssets=mulDivDown(units*buyerPrice,WAD) which is strictly less than remaining due to double floor rounding, (4) filledBuyerAssets never reaches targetFilledBuyerAssets, (5) require(filledBuyerAssets==targetFilledBuyerAssets) reverts, violating the invariant that a user who provides sufficient assets and valid offers must be able to complete the intended operation, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the user cannot execute the bundler flow for any amount that is not exactly divisible by buyerPrice/WAD? Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and buyerPrice (derived from tick via TickLib.tickToPrice) over all valid ticks and assert that mulDivDown(mulDivDown(x,WAD,p)*p,WAD)==x for all x in [1, 1e18] and all tick-derived prices p.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a buy offer (offer.buy=true) under the precondition that buyerPrice is such that mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice)*buyerPrice/WAD > remaining trigger the sequence: (1) pullToken pulls targetBuyerAssets, (2) buyerAssetsToUnits returns units=mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice), (3) Midnight.take returns resBuyerAssets=mulDivDown(units*buyerPrice,WAD) which exceeds remaining, (4) filledBuyerAssets overshoots targetFilledBuyerAssets, (5) loop exits early, (6) require(filledBuyerAssets==targetFilledBuyerAssets) reverts, violating the invariant that a taker with a valid buy offer and sufficient assets must be able to complete the bundler flow, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the user cannot use the bundler for buy offers at ticks where the mulDivUp/mulDivDown round-trip is not exact? Proof idea: unit test with offer.buy=true, tick=2910 (price near 0.5e18), targetBuyerAssets=1e6+1, assert resBuyerAssets != targetBuyerAssets-filledBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with a sell offer (offer.buy=false) under the precondition that sellerPrice does not divide targetFilledSellerAssets*WAD evenly trigger the sequence: (1) collateral is pulled and supplied, (2) sellerAssetsToUnits computes units=mulDivDown(remaining,WAD,sellerPrice), (3) Midnight.take returns resSellerAssets=mulDivUp(units*sellerPrice,WAD) which exceeds remaining due to mulDivUp in core, (4) filledSellerAssets overshoots targetFilledSellerAssets, (5) require(filledSellerAssets==targetFilledSellerAssets) reverts, violating the invariant that a seller with valid offers and sufficient collateral must be able to complete the bundler sell flow, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the user cannot sell via the bundler for amounts not exactly representable at the given tick price? Proof idea: fuzz sellerPrice over all tick-derived prices and targetSellerAssets in [1,1e18], assert mulDivUp(mulDivDown(x,WAD,p)*p,WAD)==x.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / MidnightBundles (all take loops)] Can an unprivileged taker using any bundler function with an assets-based buy offer (offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets>0) under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] is just below offer.maxAssets trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits calls buyerAssetsToUnits(midnight, id, offer, offer.maxAssets-consumed) which returns mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice), (2) Midnight.take computes newConsumed=consumed+mulDivDown(units*buyerPrice,WAD) which can equal or exceed offer.maxAssets due to the mulDivUp rounding in step 1, (3) require(newConsumed<=offer.maxAssets) reverts with ConsumedAssets(), (4) try/catch silently skips the offer, (5) targetUnits/targetAssets is never reached, (6) OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must return a value that does not cause ConsumedAssets in Midnight.take, causing scoped impact: user loss where a valid partially-consumed offer is silently skipped and the bundler operation fails? Proof idea: fuzz remaining=offer.maxAssets-consumed over [1,1e6], assert that Midnight.take with units=mulDivUp(remaining,WAD,buyerPrice) does not revert with ConsumedAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / MidnightBundles (all take loops)] Can an unprivileged taker using any bundler function with an assets-based sell offer (offer.buy=false, offer.maxAssets>0) under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] is just below offer.maxAssets trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits calls sellerAssetsToUnits(midnight, id, offer, offer.maxAssets-consumed) which returns mulDivDown(remaining,WAD,sellerPrice), (2) Midnight.take computes newConsumed=consumed+mulDivUp(units*sellerPrice,WAD) which can exceed offer.maxAssets due to mulDivUp in core, (3) ConsumedAssets() revert is caught by try/catch, (4) offer is silently skipped, (5) bundler fails with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must produce a units value that passes Midnight's consumption check, causing scoped impact: user loss where a valid near-exhausted sell offer is silently skipped and the bundler operation fails? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral vs supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged integrator or user comparing the two bundler functions under the precondition that referralFeePct=p is the same for both trigger the sequence: (1) buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral computes referralFeeAssets=filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(p,WAD-p) (formula: pct/(1-pct) of filled), (2) supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget computes referralFeeAssets=filledSellerAssets.mulDivDown(p,WAD) (formula: pct of filled), (3) for the same p and filled amount, the buy-side fee is strictly higher than the sell-side fee, violating the invariant that the referral fee formula must be symmetric and documented consistently across buy and sell paths, causing scoped impact: user loss where a buyer pays a higher effective referral fee than a seller for the same referralFeePct, leading to unexpected asset loss?
